summary.intent: Add a generic webhooks system to Moltis that supports GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and other provider triggers to run AI agents in response to external events.
summary.outcome: A complete webhooks feature was built and merged into a PR, including a new `moltis-webhooks` crate (25 passing tests), gateway/HTTP integration, web UI settings page, and comprehensive documentation.

repo: The codebase uses a service-traits pattern: define a trait + Noop impl in `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs`, then a Live impl in `crates/gateway/src/`.
GatewayState is Arc-wrapped; fields that need late initialization must be set via `Arc::get_mut` immediately after construction (before any clones), or initialized in the constructor.
RPC methods are registered in `crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs` with separate read/write authorization lists in `crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs`.
Public HTTP paths (no session auth) are exempted in `crates/httpd/src/auth_middleware.rs`.
Routes are added to the app after `finalize_gateway_app` in `crates/httpd/src/server.rs`.
The `moltis-httpd` crate has a pre-existing compile error under the vault feature (ssh_routes) that exists on main — not introduced by this work.
Gon data (server→client bootstrapping) is defined in `crates/web/src/templates.rs` as a struct serialized to JSON.
Settings sidebar icons are defined as CSS classes (e.g., `icon-globe`, `icon-activity`) in a minified style.css; `icon-link` was already used by MCP, `icon-globe` is the best fit for webhooks.

code.finding: webhook_store, webhook_rate_limiter, and webhook_worker_tx fields are added here; initialized to None/default in with_options constructor.
Public path exemptions for unauthenticated HTTP routes (like webhook ingress) are registered here.
RPC method registration point for all service methods including the 10 new webhook RPC methods.
Webhooks settings tab entry; was using icon-link (duplicate with MCP), fixed to icon-globe.

workflow: Large feature builds benefit from parallel subagents for independent file creation tasks (core crate files, store/auth, profiles).
Compile early and often: running `cargo check -p moltis-webhooks` after initial file creation caught two small issues before integration.
Verify pre-existing errors on main before attributing compile failures to new changes (`git stash` + compile check on main).
The plan file served as a living document, updated twice before implementation began — once for provider profiles and once for settings-only placement/edit/delete flows.

friction: OAuth token expiry caused the first user message to fail entirely, requiring the user to repeat their request.
GatewayState is Arc-wrapped making late field mutation require `Arc::get_mut` workaround rather than simple field assignment.
Pre-existing `moltis-httpd` vault feature compile error obscured whether httpd-level changes were correct — required checking main branch to confirm it was pre-existing.
Icon for webhooks in the settings nav was a black square (`icon-link` fallback) — required a follow-up fix after the initial implementation.
open_items: Response action tools (post GitHub comments, merge PRs, issue Stripe refunds) referenced in the plan are scaffolded but the actual tool implementations calling provider APIs are not yet built.
GitLab and Stripe profiles have basic structure but are less complete than GitHub profile — event normalization and entity key extraction need expansion.
Web UI settings page (`page-webhooks.js`) is a frontend JS file but lacks full integration testing; the deliveries inspector UI is present but not battle-tested.
The `EventFilter::accepts()` logic in `src/filter.rs` is noted as a placeholder.
Per-entity session mode with serialized processing per entity key (e.g., all events for PR #567 in one session) is described in the plan but worker implementation details may be incomplete.
Jira, PagerDuty, Sentry, and Linear profiles are listed in the plan but only GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and Generic profiles were implemented in the crate.
Webhook onboarding exclusion is documented in the plan but the actual onboarding UI code was not modified to verify it's absent.
No e2e or integration tests exist for the full HTTP ingress → worker → agent execution pipeline.
